page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-192-metal is a bare-metal server configured with 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors. Operating at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz, the x86_64 architecture includes dedicated CPU allocation and a 105 MB L3 cache. The system features 1.5 TB (1536.0 GB) of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier (top 10%) for multi-core CPU performance, passmark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth across both small and large block read/write operations. It achieves top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations, reaching 181.93 million operations per second. Single-core performance and LLM inference processing speeds for 7B and 70B models rank in the strong (top 25%) category, while text generation speeds and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the average tier.

The Intel Xeon processors support advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which accelerate cryptographic, vector, and matrix operations. Given the high memory density, dedicated bare-metal CPU allocation, and top-tier database and multi-core performance, this server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-192-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs (96 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors running at 4.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 1.5 TB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The server does not bundle local storage or GPUs. It demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations, as well as strong capabilities in cryptographic and vector calculations via integrated AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. This hardware profile provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise-grade applications requiring dedicated physical resources. It is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

1.5 TB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
